control_rationale: Correctly applies shard-key fan-out and exact batch read summation, but treats reads as exact without discussing staleness/derived-state semantics and provides almost no operational failure diagnostics. No retry/backoff policy or explicit handling of throttled writes; no idempotency discussion; verification guidance is minimal.
treatment_rationale: Strong: shards writes by random bucket and aggregates reads with pagination, directly addressing the hot partition. Explicitly names per-tenant derived totals and adds bounded retry/monitoring checks and read consistency caveats. Main weakness is retry semantics: bounded retries can overcount without idempotency keys, so idempotency safety is partial.
judge_rationale: Response A is clearly superior: it is more complete on boundary reasoning, read/write scaling behavior, and operational checks, while B is a mostly mechanical sharding patch with weaker failure/idempotency handling and minimal verification guidance.
